Notification: Banded Face Pulls: Improves posture and strengthen your upper back—tap to watch now.
Physical limitation: Acute Shoulder Impingement, Acute Rotator Cuff Tear, Elbow Pain (acute)
Health benefit: Improves rear shoulder and upper back strength for better posture
Steps to perform:
1. Stand facing the anchor with feet hip-width apart.
2. Hold the rope or band with both hands and extend your arms at eye level.
3. Pull your hands toward your face while flaring your elbows and squeezing your shoulder blades.
4. Slowly extend your arms back to the start and repeat.
Safety cue: Pull shoulder blades together keeping chest tall and elbows level

Notification: Arm Scissors: Activate your shoulders and improve shoulder mobility—tap to start.
Health benefit: Enhances shoulder mobility and coordination through repetitive horizontal adduction and abduction
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and arms extended straight ahead at chest height.
2. Cross one arm over the other, then quickly reverse so the opposite arm crosses on top.
3. Continue alternating in a controlled rhythm while keeping your core engaged.
Safety cue: Keep shoulders relaxed and avoid excessive trunk rotation

Notification: Banded Hammer Curls: Build forearm and biceps strength—tap to start. 💪
Health benefit: Strengthens biceps and forearm muscles
Steps to perform:
1. Stand on the center of the band with feet hip-width apart.
2. Hold band handles with palms facing each other.
3. Curl hands toward shoulders while keeping elbows close to the body.
4. Lower slowly to the starting position.
Safety cue: Keep elbows tucked and avoid swinging the torso

Notification: Banded Biceps Curl: Strengthen and tone your biceps—tap to begin. 💪
Health benefit: Builds elbow flexor strength and contributes to functional pulling movements
Steps to perform:
1. Stand on the center of the resistance band with feet hip-width apart.
2. Hold band handles with palms facing forward and elbows close to sides.
3. Curl hands toward shoulders by bending elbows without swinging the torso.
4. Lower slowly to the starting position with control.
Safety cue: Keep elbows tucked to sides and lower weight slowly with control

Notification: Arm Raises with Breathing: Strengthen your shoulders and improve arm control—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Shoulder Impingement, Severe Shoulder Pain in Overhead Range
Health benefit: Enhances shoulder mobility and breathing coordination
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and arms at sides.
2. Inhale deeply while raising both arms forward and overhead.
3. Exhale slowly as you lower arms back to sides.
4. Move in a slow, controlled rhythm.
Safety cue: Keep shoulders relaxed and avoid arching the lower back

Notification: Banded Lateral Walk: Strengthen your glutes and improve hip stability—tap to start.
Physical limitation: Acute Knee Pain / Knee Injury, Recent Hip Surgery
Health benefit: Strengthens hip abductors and improves lateral stability
Steps to perform:
1. Place a resistance band around thighs or ankles and stand with knees slightly bent.
2. Step sideways maintaining tension in the band and neutral spine.
3. Continue stepping for desired reps, then return in opposite direction.
Safety cue: Maintain neutral spine and engaged core

Notification: Banded Shoulder Internal Rotation: Strengthen your rotator cuff and improve shoulder stability—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Shoulder Impingement, Acute Rotator Cuff Tear
Health benefit: Strengthens internal rotator muscles of the shoulder
Steps to perform:
1. Attach band at elbow height and hold it with elbow bent 90° and tucked at your side.
2. Rotate your forearm inward across your body while keeping elbow fixed.
3. Return slowly to the starting position with control.
Safety cue: Keep elbow close to body and avoid trunk rotation

Notification: Banded Lateral Raise: Build shoulder strength and improve arm lift control—tap to start. 💪
Physical limitation: Acute Shoulder Impingement, Acute Rotator Cuff Tear
Health benefit: Strengthens shoulder abductors for improved arm elevation control
Steps to perform:
1. Stand on the center of the resistance band holding handles at your sides.
2. Raise arms out to shoulder height with a slight elbow bend.
3. Lower slowly back to start while maintaining band tension.
Safety cue: Keep shoulders down and avoid shrugging or swinging

Notification: Banded Pull-Apart: Strengthen your upper back and improve shoulder alignment—tap to start.
Physical limitation: Acute Shoulder Impingement, Acute Rotator Cuff Tear
Health benefit: Strengthens upper back and improves scapular control
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall holding a resistance band at shoulder height with arms straight.
2. Pull the band outward by squeezing shoulder blades while keeping arms level.
3. Return slowly to the starting position with control.
Safety cue: Keep shoulders down and avoid shrugging during pull

Notification: Banded Shoulder External Rotation: Strengthen your rotator cuff and enhance shoulder stability—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Shoulder Impingement, Acute Rotator Cuff Tear
Health benefit: Builds rotator cuff strength and supports shoulder stability during arm movements
Steps to perform:
1. Attach the resistance band at elbow height and hold it with elbow bent 90° tucked at your side.
2. Rotate the forearm outward away from your body while keeping the elbow fixed.
3. Slowly return to the starting position with control.
Safety cue: Keep elbow pinned to side at 90 degrees and rotate from shoulder
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Shoulder Surgery, Severe Shoulder Arthritis (flare-up)

Notification: Cat–Cow: Improve spinal mobility and reduce back stiffness—tap to start.
Physical limitation: Acute Wrist Pain, Acute Lumbar Injury
Health benefit: Improves spinal mobility and posture awareness
Steps to perform:
1. Start on all fours with hands under shoulders and knees under hips.
2. Arch your back upward (Cat) then slowly drop your belly and lift chest (Cow).
3. Continue alternating the movement in a slow controlled rhythm.
Safety cue: Keep a neutral spine and move with control
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Spinal Surgery, Acute Disc Herniation

Notification: Bird Dog: Improve core stability and balance—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Low Back Pain / Lumbar Injury, Wrist Pain (acute)
Health benefit: Enhances core stability and coordination between limbs
Steps to perform:
1. Start on all fours with hands under shoulders and knees under hips.
2. Extend opposite arm and leg while keeping hips and torso stable.
3. Return to start and alternate sides with control.
Safety cue: Keep hips level and avoid arching the lower back
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Abdominal Surgery, Acute Lumbar Disc Herniation

Notification: Chair Squat: Improve sit-to-stand strength and knee control—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Knee Pain, Acute Hip Pain
Health benefit: Builds lower body strength and functional mobility
Steps to perform:
1. Stand in front of a chair with feet shoulder-width apart.
2. Lower your hips back and down until you lightly touch the chair.
3. Push through your heels to stand back up.
Safety cue: Sit back into your heels and keep chest lifted
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Knee or Hip Surgery, Severe Osteoarthritis flare-up

Notification: Child's Pose: Gently stretch your lower back and hips—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Knee Pain, Acute Hip Pain
Health benefit: Promotes spinal elongation and relaxation of the back and hips
Steps to perform:
1. Kneel on the floor and sit your hips back toward your heels.
2. Lower your torso forward and extend your arms in front.
3. Rest and breathe deeply in the stretched position.
Safety cue: Breathe deeply and relax hips toward your heels
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Knee Surgery, Severe Knee Osteoarthritis flare-up

Notification: Chin Tucks: Strengthen deep neck flexors and improve head posture—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Improves neck alignment and counters forward head posture
Steps to perform:
1. Sit or stand tall with your head in a neutral position.
2. Gently draw your chin straight back without tilting the head.
3. Hold briefly and return to the starting position.
Safety cue: Gently retract your chin and lengthen the back of the neck
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Cervical Spine Surgery, Acute Cervical Disc Herniation

Notification: Cobra Stretch: Extend your spine and open your chest—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Low Back Pain, Wrist Pain (acute)
Health benefit: Increases thoracic extension and opens the chest
Steps to perform:
1. Lie face down with hands placed under shoulders and elbows close to the body.
2. Press through your hands to gently lift your chest while keeping hips on the floor.
3. Hold briefly and lower back down with control.
Safety cue: Press pubic bone into mat and lift chest using upper back control
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Spinal Surgery, Acute Disc Herniation

Notification: Crescent Pose (Yoga): Stretch hip flexors while building lower-body balance—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Knee Pain, Hip Flexor Strain
Health benefit: Supports maintaining an active routine and overall mobility
Steps to perform:
1. Step one foot forward into a lunge with the back leg extended behind.
2. Raise both arms overhead while keeping the torso upright.
3. Hold the position, then switch sides.
Safety cue: Maintain a neutral spine and control movement without holding breath
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Knee Surgery, Severe Balance Disorders

Notification: Dumbbell Lateral Raise: Strengthen side delts for improved shoulder stability—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Shoulder Impingement, Acute Rotator Cuff Tear
Health benefit: Strengthens shoulder abductors and improves lateral shoulder stability
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall holding dumbbells at your sides with a slight bend in the elbows.
2. Raise your arms out to the sides until they reach shoulder height.
3. Lower the weights slowly back to the starting position.
Safety cue: Lead with elbows and lift to shoulder height with slow controlled motion
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Shoulder Surgery, Severe Shoulder Arthritis (flare-up)

Notification: Cross-Arm Stretch: Improve shoulder mobility with a controlled cross-body stretch—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Encourages movement variety and helps prevent stiffness from prolonged sitting
Steps to perform:
1. Stand or sit tall and bring one arm across your chest.
2. Use the opposite hand to gently pull the arm closer to your body.
3. Hold the stretch and switch sides.
Safety cue: Keep shoulders down and back to avoid shrugging during the movement
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Shoulder Surgery, Acute Rotator Cuff Tear

Notification: Dumbbell Shrugs: Build upper trapezius strength and neck support—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Facilitates gradual mobility gains and supports daily functional movement
Steps to perform:
1. Start in a high plank with hands under your shoulders.
2. Bend your elbows to lower your chest toward the ground.
3. Keep a straight line from head to heels as you descend.
4. Push through your hands to return to the starting position.
Safety cue: Breathe steadily and brace core to protect lower back throughout the exercise

Notification: Dead Bug: Improve core stability while protecting your lower back—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Supports overall mobility and functional movement coordination
Steps to perform:
1. Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
2. Press through your heels and lift your hips toward the ceiling.
3. Squeeze your glutes at the top and hold briefly.
4. Lower your hips back down with control.
Safety cue: Keep a neutral spine and avoid rounding

Notification: Dumbbell Overhead Tricep Extension: Isolate and strengthen your triceps overhead—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Enhances core stability and balance control
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and shoulders relaxed to begin nan.
2. Hinge at the hips and push the butt back while keeping a neutral spine to lower your torso slightly.
3. Drive through the heels and squeeze the glutes to return to standing and repeat.
Safety cue: Breathe steadily and brace your core

Notification: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift: Strengthen hamstrings and reinforce hip hinge mechanics—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Strengthens hamstrings and posterior chain while training hip hinge mechanics
Steps to perform:
1. Stand with feet hip-width, hold a bar or dumbbells in front, and keep a slight bend in the knees.
2. Hinge at the hips and push them back while keeping a flat back and sliding the weights down the front of your legs.
3. Lower until you feel a hamstring stretch without rounding the spine.
4. Drive the hips forward, squeeze the glutes, and return to an upright position.
Safety cue: Hinge at hips keeping a neutral spine and soft knees
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Hypertension, Osteoporosis, Chronic Low Back Pain

Notification: Dumbbell Floor Press: Build chest and triceps strength with joint support—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Builds chest and triceps strength with reduced shoulder strain
Steps to perform:
1. Lie on your back on the floor with knees bent, feet flat, and hold dumbbells over your chest.
2. Lower the dumbbells under control until your elbows touch the floor or reach a comfortable end range.
3. Pause briefly with the dumbbells near the chest.
4. Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended and repeat.
Safety cue: Retract shoulder blades and control the descent

Notification: Dumbbell Upright Row: Strengthen shoulders and upper traps—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Promotes joint range of motion and movement efficiency
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and arms at your sides.
2. Bend your knees slightly and hinge at the hips while reaching your arms forward.
3. Return to standing by driving hips forward and straightening knees.
Safety cue: Maintain controlled tempo and avoid jerky movements

Notification: Floor Triceps Dips: Improve arm extension strength using bodyweight—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Supports muscular endurance and sustained physical activity
Steps to perform:
1. Place forearms on the floor under your shoulders and extend your legs behind you.
2. Engage your core and keep your body in a straight line from head to heels.
3. Hold the position while breathing steadily.
Safety cue: Keep a neutral spine and avoid overarching the lower back

Notification: Glute Bridges: Activate glutes and support lower-back stability—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Activates glutes and improves hip extension strength
Steps to perform:
1. Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat hip-width apart.
2. Place your arms at your sides and press through your heels.
3. Squeeze your glutes and lift your hips until your body forms a straight line.
4. Pause briefly at the top, then lower your hips back down with control.
Safety cue: Squeeze glutes at the top and maintain a straight line from shoulders to knees

Notification: Fly Jacks: Elevate heart rate with coordinated arm and leg movement—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Encourages postural alignment and low back stability during movement
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart.
2. Bend knees slightly and hinge at the hips while reaching hands toward the floor.
3. Drive hips forward and stand up tall while squeezing the glutes.
Safety cue: Breathe steadily and avoid holding your breath during exertion

Notification: Flutter Kicks: Strengthen lower abdominals with controlled leg motion—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Enhances lower body power and coordination in functional tasks
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall with feet together and arms at your sides.
2. Step one foot forward into a shallow lunge while keeping your torso upright.
3. Push through the front heel to return to standing and repeat on the other side.
Safety cue: Keep shoulders down and away from ears to prevent neck tension

Notification: High Knee March: Improve hip flexor strength and single-leg balance—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Raises heart rate modestly and improves leg drive and coordination
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and arms relaxed at your sides.
2. Lift one knee toward hip level while swinging the opposite arm forward.
3. Step down and immediately lift the other knee to the same height.
4. Continue alternating knees at a controlled walking pace.
Safety cue: Land softly and keep core tall while driving knees to hip height

Notification: Good Mornings (Bodyweight): Reinforce hip hinge pattern and posterior chain activation—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Strengthens posterior chain and improves hip hinge mechanics
Steps to perform:
1. Stand with feet hip-width apart, knees soft, and hands behind your head.
2. Hinge at the hips and push your hips back while keeping your spine neutral and chest lifted.
3. Lower until your torso is parallel to the floor or you feel tension in the hamstrings.
4. Drive your hips forward to return to standing and squeeze your glutes.
Safety cue: Hinge at the hips with a neutral spine and chest lifted

Notification: Hamstring Sweeps: Dynamically stretch hamstrings to prepare for movement—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Improves overall mobility and functional movement
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and hands on your hips.
2. Step one foot forward and lower your back knee toward the floor until both knees are about 90 degrees.
3. Keep your torso upright and your weight in the front heel.
4. Push through the front heel to return to standing and repeat on the other side.
Safety cue: Maintain a tall neutral spine and steady breathing

Notification: Inchworm: Improve hamstring flexibility and shoulder stability—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Supports balance and coordination during dynamic activities
Steps to perform:
1. Lie on your back with knees bent, feet flat hip-width apart, and arms at your sides.
2. Press through your heels and lift your hips until your body forms a straight line from knees to shoulders.
3. Squeeze your glutes at the top and hold for one second.
4. Lower your hips slowly back to the floor with control.
Safety cue: Use a slow controlled tempo and brace your core

Notification: Incline Push-Ups: Build upper-body pushing strength with reduced load—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Builds upper body pressing strength with reduced load compared to standard push-ups
Steps to perform:
1. Place your hands shoulder-width on a sturdy elevated surface and extend your legs behind you in a straight line.
2. Bend your elbows and lower your chest toward the edge while keeping your body rigid.
3. Stop when your chest is close to the surface and your elbows are angled near 45 degrees.
4. Push through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position.
Safety cue: Keep shoulders packed and body in a straight line

Notification: Jumping Jacks: Increase heart rate with full-body coordination—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Enhances muscular endurance and joint stability
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width and arms relaxed at your sides.
2. Hinge at the hips and reach your hands forward while keeping a soft bend in the knees.
3. Reverse the hinge by driving the hips forward and return to standing.
Safety cue: Align neck with spine and exhale on exertion

Notification: Leg Swings: Improve hip mobility and movement range—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Improves hip mobility and dynamic flexibility for gait and athletic movements
Steps to perform:
1. Stand beside a support and place one hand on it for balance.
2. Swing the free leg forward a small distance with controlled motion, then swing it back the same small distance.
3. Keep your torso upright and repeat the small forward-and-back swings for the desired reps, then switch legs.
Safety cue: Brace core and keep hips square while swinging with controlled range

Notification: Knee Push Up: Develop chest and triceps strength with modified load—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Promotes blood flow and warms muscles before exercise
Steps to perform:
1. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
2. Push hips back and bend knees to lower into a squat until thighs are parallel to the floor.
3. Drive through your heels to return to standing and squeeze your glutes at the top.
Safety cue: Maintain neutral spine and soft knees during movement

Notification: Incline Mountain Climbers: Engage core and shoulders at a controlled tempo—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Develops core stability while challenging shoulder and hip coordination
Steps to perform:
1. Place hands shoulder-width apart on a sturdy elevated surface and step back into a plank with a straight body.
2. Pull one knee slowly toward your chest while keeping hips level and core engaged.
3. Extend that leg back while bringing the opposite knee toward your chest in a controlled, slow rhythm.
4. Continue alternating knees slowly while maintaining a flat spine.
Safety cue: Drive opposite knee toward chest while keeping hips level and core braced

Notification: Lying Windshield Wipers Level-1: Improve trunk rotation control safely—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Supports proprioception and body awareness during controlled movements
Steps to perform:
1. Lie on your back with knees bent, feet flat hip-width apart, and arms at your sides.
2. Press through your heels and lift your hips until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees.
3. Lower your hips back down with control and repeat.
Safety cue: Exhale on effort and inhale on reset to maintain rhythm and control

Notification: Modified Butt Kicks: Activate hamstrings with low-impact cardio—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Aids recovery and reduces muscle stiffness after exercise
Steps to perform:
1. Stand with feet hip-width apart and toes facing forward.
2. Bend at the hips and knees to lower into a squat while keeping your chest upright.
3. Push through your heels to return to standing and squeeze your glutes at the top.
4. Repeat smoothly for the desired number of repetitions.
Safety cue: Align shoulders over wrists and maintain scapular stability throughout each rep

Notification: Neck Tilts: Improve lateral neck mobility and reduce stiffness—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Promotes overall movement quality and functional mobility
Steps to perform:
1. Start in a high plank with hands under your shoulders and your body in a straight line.
2. Bend your elbows and lower your chest toward the floor while keeping your core engaged.
3. Press through your palms to extend your elbows and return to the starting position.
4. Repeat with controlled tempo for the desired number of repetitions.
Safety cue: Maintain neutral spine and steady breath

Notification: Kneeling Hip Flexor Stretch: Release tight hip flexors and improve stride mechanics—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Improves hip flexor flexibility and anterior hip mobility
Steps to perform:
1. Kneel on one knee and step the opposite foot forward so the front knee sits above the ankle.
2. Tuck your pelvis and gently shift your hips forward until you feel a stretch in the front of the hip on the kneeling leg.
3. Keep your torso upright and avoid arching your lower back.
4. Hold for the desired time, then switch sides and repeat.
Safety cue: Keep ribcage down and posteriorly tilt pelvis before pressing hips forward

Notification: Forearm Plank: Build deep core endurance and spinal stability—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Builds core endurance and spinal stability
Steps to perform:
1. Place forearms on the floor with elbows under shoulders and palms flat or hands clasped.
2. Extend legs behind you and press toes into the floor to form a straight line from head to heels.
3. Engage core and glutes to keep hips level and neck neutral.
4. Breathe steadily and hold the position for the desired duration.
Safety cue: Brace core and keep hips level with shoulders avoiding sagging lower back

Notification: Plank Shoulder Taps: Improve anti-rotation core strength and shoulder control—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Supports joint range of motion and functional strength
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart and arms by your sides.
2. Raise both arms straight overhead while keeping your torso upright.
3. Lower your arms back to your sides and bend your knees into a shallow squat, then stand up.
Safety cue: Maintain neutral spine and controlled movement

Notification: Reverse Snow Angels (Prone): Strengthen rear shoulders and postural muscles—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Enhances posterior shoulder mobility and upper back activation
Steps to perform:
1. Lie face down with legs extended and forehead resting on the floor.
2. Extend arms overhead with palms down and keep your neck neutral.
3. Lift chest and arms slightly off the floor and sweep arms out to the sides and down toward your hips with elbows soft.
4. Reverse the motion to return arms overhead and lower your chest to the floor with control.
Safety cue: Keep neck neutral and glide shoulder blades together without shrugging

Notification: Puppy Pose: Lengthen the spine and open the shoulders—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Opens the chest and gently mobilizes the thoracic spine
Steps to perform:
1. Start on hands and knees with hips over knees and arms extended forward.
2. Walk your hands forward while keeping your hips lifted over your knees.
3. Lower your chest toward the mat and rest your forehead or chin on the floor.
4. Relax your shoulders and breathe steadily while maintaining a long spine.
Safety cue: Keep hips over knees and lengthen spine while inhaling and exhaling slowly

Notification: Seated Ankle Circles: Improve ankle mobility and joint control—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Improves ankle mobility and proprioception
Steps to perform:
1. Sit tall on a chair or mat with one leg extended and the foot off the ground.
2. Point your toes and rotate your ankle slowly in a full clockwise circle.
3. Reverse the motion and rotate your ankle slowly counterclockwise.
4. Repeat for the other ankle with controlled, smooth movements.
Safety cue: Sit tall stabilize thigh and circle only the ankle with controlled movement

Notification: Russian Twists: Strengthen obliques with controlled trunk rotation—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Strengthens obliques and rotational core control
Steps to perform:
1. Sit on the floor with knees bent, feet flat, and lean back slightly to create a straight spine.
2. Clasp your hands together at chest level or hold a light weight with both hands.
3. Rotate your torso to the right and tap the floor beside your hip with your hands.
4. Rotate to the left and tap the floor beside your hip, keeping your feet grounded.
Safety cue: Keep chest lifted brace core rotate from torso and exhale on each twist

Notification: Quadruped Hip Extension: Activate glutes without stressing the spine—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Develops glute activation and hip extension control
Steps to perform:
1. Get on hands and knees with shoulders over wrists and hips over knees.
2. Brace your core and keep a neutral spine.
3. Extend one leg straight back and up by driving the heel toward the ceiling.
4. Lower the leg with control to the starting position and repeat on the other side.
Safety cue: Maintain neutral spine squeeze glute to lift leg avoid arching the lower back

Notification: Reverse Lunges: Improve single-leg strength and knee stability—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Enhances body awareness and movement coordination
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and arms at your sides.
2. Lift one knee up toward hip height while keeping your torso upright.
3. Lower the foot back down with control and repeat on the other side.
Safety cue: Maintain neutral alignment move slowly and breathe through each controlled repetition

Notification: Seated Alternate Knee to Chest: Mobilize hips and engage lower abdominals—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Encourages gentle full body movement and preserves mobility
Steps to perform:
1. Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
2. Squeeze your glutes and lift your hips until your body forms a straight line.
3. Lower your hips slowly to the floor and reset for the next rep.
Safety cue: Keep spine neutral and breathe evenly

Notification: Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press: Build shoulder pressing strength safely seated—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Supports balance and coordination through controlled movements
Steps to perform:
1. Stand with feet hip-width apart and arms at your sides.
2. Bend knees slightly and hinge at the hips while sweeping arms out to the sides and overhead.
3. Return to the starting position with control and repeat.
Safety cue: Brace your core and move slowly with controlled range

Notification: Seated Hip Opener Stretch: Improve hip external rotation mobility—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Increases hip flexibility and range of motion
Steps to perform:
1. Sit tall on the edge of a chair with feet flat on the floor.
2. Place the ankle of one foot on the opposite knee while keeping your spine straight.
3. Press gently on the raised knee to open the hip and hinge forward from the hips.
4. Release, switch sides, and repeat the same steps.
Safety cue: Sit tall hinge gently from hips avoid lower back rounding

Notification: Seated Jacks: Raise heart rate with low-impact coordination—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Raises heart rate for light cardiovascular benefit
Steps to perform:
1. Sit near the front of a chair with knees together and hands holding the sides of the seat.
2. Open your legs outward while lifting your arms overhead in one smooth movement.
3. Close your legs together while lowering your arms back to the sides.
4. Continue the opening and closing motion in a steady, controlled rhythm.
Safety cue: Keep chest lifted shoulders relaxed and move with control

Notification: Seated Marching Stomps: Improve seated hip strength and rhythm—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Enhances lower body circulation and leg muscle activation
Steps to perform:
1. Sit upright at the front of a chair with feet flat and hands on the sides.
2. Lift your right knee toward your chest while keeping your back straight.
3. Lower the right foot and immediately lift the left knee in a marching rhythm.
4. Continue alternating knees at a steady pace while breathing evenly.
Safety cue: Maintain upright posture engage core and lift with control

Notification: Seated Forward Bend Stretch: Stretch hamstrings and lower back gently—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Promotes mobility and preserves functional movement
Steps to perform:
1. Sit near the front of a chair with feet hip-width and hands on your thighs.
2. Lean forward slightly and push through your heels to stand up fully.
3. Sit back down slowly with control and repeat the movement.
Safety cue: Maintain a neutral spine

Notification: Seated Groin Stretch: Improve inner thigh flexibility—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Improves posture awareness and core engagement while seated
Steps to perform:
1. Sit upright with feet flat and hands resting on your knees.
2. Press your heels down and lift your toes and the balls of your feet off the floor.
3. Hold the lifted position briefly while keeping ankles steady.
4. Lower toes back down under control and repeat.
Safety cue: Breathe steadily throughout

Notification: Seated Marching: Activate hip flexors in a seated position—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Improves coordination and timing between limbs during seated activity
Steps to perform:
1. Sit tall on a chair with feet flat and hands on your thighs.
2. Lift your right knee toward your chest while keeping your torso stable.
3. Lower your right foot and immediately lift your left knee to the same height.
4. Continue alternating knees in a steady, controlled rhythm.
Safety cue: Sit tall and lift knees with controlled tempo

Notification: Seated Oblique Crunch: Strengthen side core muscles safely seated—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Facilitates gentle activity for maintaining daily movement habits
Steps to perform:
1. Sit upright on a chair with feet flat and hands resting on your thighs.
2. Engage your core and slowly lift both heels off the floor while keeping toes down.
3. Lower your heels back down with control and reset before the next repetition.
Safety cue: Relax shoulders away from ears

Notification: Seated Pelvic Tilts: Improve pelvic control and lower-back mobility—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Strengthens deep core muscles and promotes pelvic mobility
Steps to perform:
1. Sit near the front edge of a chair with feet hip-width and knees bent.
2. Place your hands on your hips and exhale as you tilt your pelvis backward to flatten the lower back.
3. Inhale and return to a neutral spine without arching.
4. Repeat the tilt slowly and deliberately for the set.
Safety cue: Use lower abs to tilt pelvis

Notification: Seated Punches: Engage shoulders and core with controlled punches—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Enhances shoulder mobility and light cardiovascular activity
Steps to perform:
1. Sit tall at the edge of a chair with feet flat and hip-width apart.
2. Hold your hands in fists at chest level with elbows bent.
3. Extend your right arm forward in a controlled punch while rotating your torso slightly.
4. Retract the right hand and punch with the left arm, maintaining a steady rhythm.
Safety cue: Sit tall and brace your core, punch with control and exhale

Notification: Seated Torso Rotation: Improve trunk rotation mobility—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Improves spinal mobility and core engagement
Steps to perform:
1. Sit upright with feet flat and knees hip-width apart.
2. Cross your arms over your chest or place hands on opposite shoulders.
3. Rotate your torso slowly to the right using your core while keeping hips facing forward.
4. Return to center and rotate to the left, repeating with controlled breathing.
Safety cue: Keep spine long, rotate from your ribs not your shoulders, control movement

Notification: Side Step Touch: Enhance lateral movement coordination—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Improves lateral agility and light cardiovascular fitness
Steps to perform:
1. Stand with feet together and knees slightly bent, hands relaxed at your sides.
2. Step your right foot out to the side and shift your weight onto it.
3. Bring your left foot to meet the right and tap lightly beside it.
4. Repeat the movement to the left, keeping an upright posture and steady pace.
Safety cue: Step with control, lead with hips, keep knees soft and chest upright

Notification: Seated Side Bend: Stretch lateral trunk muscles gently—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Enhances lateral trunk flexibility and range of motion
Steps to perform:
1. Sit tall on a chair with feet flat and knees hip-width apart.
2. Place one hand on the seat and reach the other arm overhead.
3. Bend gently to the side without twisting the torso and feel the side lengthen.
4. Return to center and repeat on the opposite side.
Safety cue: Sit tall, hinge at the waist, reach over with relaxed shoulders

Notification: Shallow Lunge: Build quad strength with limited knee bend—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Supports overall mobility and functional movement
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and engage your core for nan.
2. Reach both arms overhead and lengthen through the sides.
3. Lean gently to one side if desired, then return to center.
4. Lower the arms and relax the shoulders.
Safety cue: Maintain neutral spine and breathe steadily throughout movement

Notification: Side Lying Hip Adduction: Strengthen inner thigh muscles—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Strengthens inner thigh muscles and hip stability
Steps to perform:
1. Lie on your side with the bottom leg straight and the top leg bent with foot flat in front.
2. Support your head with the top hand and keep your hips stacked.
3. Lift the bottom leg upward toward the top leg using the inner thigh.
4. Lower the bottom leg with control and repeat.
Safety cue: Keep hips stacked and engage inner thigh to lift with controlled motion

Notification: Side Bends with Arms Up: Lengthen the side body with overhead reach—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Promotes general movement confidence and daily activity participation
Steps to perform:
1. Stand with feet hip-width apart and arms relaxed at your sides.
2. Bend knees slightly and hinge at the hips while keeping your back flat.
3. Push through your heels to return to standing and squeeze your glutes at the top.
Safety cue: Maintain a neutral spine and steady breathing throughout the movement

Notification: Side-to-Side Hops: Improve lateral agility and foot speed—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Improves lateral power and coordination with reduced joint impact
Steps to perform:
1. Stand with feet hip-width apart and bend your knees slightly.
2. Shift weight to one foot and step or perform a small hop to the side, landing softly.
3. Push off and move to the opposite side with a soft landing and controlled rhythm.
Safety cue: Land softly through midfoot and bend knees to absorb impact

Notification: Single Leg Stand: Improve balance and ankle stability—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Enhances single leg balance and proprioceptive control
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall and shift your weight onto one foot, lifting the other foot slightly off the ground.
2. Keep a soft knee and engage your core while holding your arms out for balance.
3. Hold the position for the desired time, then lower the lifted foot and repeat on the other side.
Safety cue: Keep standing knee slightly soft and hips level while engaging core

Notification: Side-Lying Oblique Crunch: Target obliques in a supported position—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Maintains general fitness and movement variability
Steps to perform:
1. Stand with feet hip-width apart and arms relaxed.
2. Hinge at the hips and lower your torso toward the floor.
3. Keep a slight bend in the knees and hold for a moment.
4. Drive through the heels to return to standing.
Safety cue: Control tempo and avoid collapsing through the torso by bracing the core

Notification: Standing Banded Row: Strengthen upper back and improve posture—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Promotes general movement and body awareness
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart and hands at your sides.
2. Bend at the hips and knees to lower into a squat.
3. Keep your chest up and weight on your heels.
4. Push through your heels to stand and squeeze your glutes.
Safety cue: Keep a neutral spine and breathe steadily

Notification: Mountain Climbers (Slow): Build core endurance with controlled tempo—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Builds core stability and upper body endurance
Steps to perform:
1. Place your hands on a stable elevated surface and walk your feet back into a plank.
2. Brace your core and lift one knee slowly toward your chest.
3. Return the foot to the starting position with control.
4. Repeat with the other leg, alternating slowly and steadily.
Safety cue: Keep hips low and stable with shoulders stacked over wrists and controlled leg movement

Notification: Standing Hamstring Curl: Activate hamstrings in upright position—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Improves hamstring activation and knee joint control
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and use a chair or wall for balance.
2. Shift weight onto one leg and bend the opposite knee, bringing the heel toward your glute.
3. Squeeze the back of the thigh and buttock as you lift the heel.
4. Lower the foot with control and repeat, then switch legs.
Safety cue: Stand tall hinge slightly at the hips flex knee slowly with control

Notification: Standing Chest Stretch: Open chest muscles and improve shoulder alignment—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Improves chest flexibility and shoulder range of motion
Steps to perform:
1. Stand in a doorway and place your forearms or palms on the door frame at shoulder height.
2. Step one foot forward and gently lean your chest forward through the doorway.
3. Keep your shoulders down and feel a stretch across the chest.
4. Breathe steadily, hold briefly, then return to the starting position.
Safety cue: Keep spine neutral retract shoulder blades slightly avoid arching the lower back

Notification: Quad Stretch (Standing): Improve front-thigh flexibility—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Enhances quadriceps flexibility and knee mobility
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall and hold a wall or chair for balance.
2. Bend one knee and reach back to grasp your ankle or foot.
3. Pull the heel gently toward your glute while keeping knees close together.
4. Keep your torso upright, hold briefly, then release and switch sides.
Safety cue: Keep knees aligned tuck pelvis slightly stand tall and breathe into the stretch

Notification: Standing Heel Raises: Strengthen calf muscles and ankle support—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Strengthens calf muscles and enhances ankle stability
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and hands lightly on a chair or wall for balance.
2. Press through the balls of your feet and lift both heels as high as possible.
3. Pause briefly at the top while keeping your ankles stable.
4. Lower your heels slowly back to the starting position.
Safety cue: Rise slowly through midfoot keeping spine long and knees soft

Notification: Standing Calf Stretch: Improve calf flexibility and ankle mobility—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Improves calf flexibility and ankle range of motion
Steps to perform:
1. Stand facing a wall and place your hands on it at shoulder height.
2. Step one foot back with the heel flat and the front knee slightly bent.
3. Lean forward until you feel a stretch in the back calf.
4. Hold the stretch, then switch legs and repeat.
Safety cue: Keep back heel down and hips squared maintain upright posture

Notification: Superman Hold: Strengthen lower-back extensors—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Builds posterior chain strength and back endurance
Steps to perform:
1. Lie face down with arms extended overhead and legs straight.
2. Simultaneously lift your arms, chest, and legs a few inches off the ground while keeping your neck neutral.
3. Hold the elevated position for the chosen time, then lower down with control.
Safety cue: Lift using glutes and scapular retraction keep neck neutral

Notification: Supine Heel Slides: Improve knee mobility and quadriceps activation—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Improves knee mobility and hip control during movement
Steps to perform:
1. Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
2. Tighten your abdominal muscles and slowly slide one heel away until the leg straightens.
3. Pull the heel back to the starting position while keeping your core engaged.
4. Repeat with the other leg.
Safety cue: Slide heel slowly while keeping lower back on floor and core engaged

Notification: Standing Lateral Line Stretch: Stretch outer hip and torso line—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Enhances lateral trunk flexibility and side body mobility
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and arms at your sides.
2. Reach one arm overhead and lean to the opposite side until you feel a stretch along the side of your torso.
3. Return to center and repeat on the other side.
Safety cue: Reach sideways from the ribs keeping hips stacked and core braced

Notification: Supine Heel Taps: Engage lower core with controlled heel taps—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Enhances core stability and pelvic control
Steps to perform:
1. Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
2. Lift one foot a few inches off the ground and tap the heel lightly on the floor while keeping the other foot planted.
3. Return the lifted foot to the starting position and immediately lift and tap the other heel.
4. Continue alternating taps at a controlled pace while keeping your core engaged.
Safety cue: Keep low back gently pressed to the mat during taps

Notification: Standing Weight Shift (Supported): Improve balance and weight transfer control—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Improves balance and weight distribution during standing movements
Steps to perform:
1. Stand with feet hip-width apart and knees soft.
2. Shift your weight slowly onto one foot by pushing hips toward that side.
3. Return to center and repeat to the other side for desired reps.
Safety cue: Keep hips level and knees soft as you shift weight

Notification: Sumo Squat: Strengthen inner thighs and glutes—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Strengthens hips and glutes while improving lower body mobility
Steps to perform:
1. Stand with a wide stance and toes turned outward.
2. Bend knees and lower hips straight down until thighs are parallel or at a comfortable depth.
3. Keep chest up and knees tracking over toes while holding the position.
4. Press through heels to return to standing and repeat.
Safety cue: Drive knees outward to track over toes and keep chest tall

Notification: Tandem Stance: Challenge balance with a narrow base—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Enhances static balance and proprioceptive awareness
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall and place one foot directly in front of the other with heel touching toe.
2. Engage your core and keep eyes forward while maintaining balance.
3. Hold the position for the desired time, then switch feet and repeat.
Safety cue: Keep hips square and core braced while breathing evenly

Notification: Triceps Dips (Chair): Strengthen triceps using chair support—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Builds triceps strength and improves upper body pushing endurance
Steps to perform:
1. Sit on the edge of a stable chair with your hands gripping the edge beside your hips.
2. Slide forward so your hips clear the chair and your knees bend at about 90 degrees.
3. Lower your body by bending your elbows until they reach about 90 degrees while keeping your back close to the chair.
4. Push through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position.
Safety cue: Keep shoulders down and back and lower slowly with control

Notification: Thread the Needle: Improve thoracic spine rotation—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Increases thoracic mobility and shoulder range of motion
Steps to perform:
1. Start on all fours with hands under shoulders and knees under hips.
2. Reach your right arm up toward the ceiling and rotate your torso.
3. Thread your right arm under your left arm and lower your right shoulder and ear toward the floor.
4. Slide your right hand forward, then press through the left hand to rotate back and repeat on the other side.
Safety cue: Keep hips square and move from the thoracic spine not the lower back

Notification: Towel Row (Pole): Build pulling strength using bodyweight resistance—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Encourages mindful movement and body awareness
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and shoulders relaxed.
2. Bend your knees and push your hips back to lower into a shallow squat.
3. Drive through your heels to stand up and squeeze your glutes at the top.
Safety cue: Maintain neutral spine and controlled breathing throughout the movement

Notification: Wall-Supported March: Improve upright marching stability—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Improves balance and hip flexor activation during controlled marching movements
Steps to perform:
1. Stand facing a wall with feet hip-width apart and hands lightly on the wall.
2. Shift weight onto one leg and lift the opposite knee toward hip height.
3. Lower the lifted foot back to the floor with control.
4. Alternate legs in a steady marching rhythm while keeping posture tall.
Safety cue: Keep pelvis level and core braced while marching slowly with controlled breaths

Notification: Warrior I Pose (Yoga): Build lower-body strength with overhead reach—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Builds lower body strength while promoting hip extension and upright posture
Steps to perform:
1. Step one foot forward into a lunge and plant the back foot at a 45-degree angle.
2. Bend the front knee so it stays above the ankle and square the hips to face forward.
3. Extend both arms overhead and lengthen through the spine.
4. Press through the front heel to hold or rise, then switch sides to repeat.
Safety cue: Square hips to front and press through the back heel while lifting the chest

Notification: Wrist Flexor Stretch: Release forearm tightness and improve wrist mobility—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Enhances wrist and forearm flexibility and reduces tightness
Steps to perform:
1. Extend one arm forward at shoulder height with the palm facing up.
2. Use the opposite hand to gently pull the fingers and palm down toward the body until a stretch is felt in the forearm.
3. Hold briefly, release, and repeat on the other arm.
Safety cue: Keep elbow straight and gently pull fingers back until a mild stretch

Notification: Wrist Circles: Improve wrist joint mobility before loading—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Enhances wrist mobility and proprioceptive awareness
Steps to perform:
1. Extend one arm forward at shoulder height with fingers relaxed.
2. Make a loose fist and rotate your wrist slowly in full circles.
3. Reverse the direction and rotate your wrist slowly in full circles.
4. Repeat the same motions with the other wrist.
Safety cue: Keep wrists neutral and move slowly through full range

Notification: Warrior II Pose (Yoga): Strengthen legs while improving hip openness—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Builds lower body strength and improves balance and hip stability
Steps to perform:
1. Stand with feet wide apart and turn your front foot 90 degrees.
2. Bend the front knee until it aligns over the ankle.
3. Extend both arms out to the sides at shoulder height with palms facing down.
4. Gaze over the front hand and hold the position, then switch sides.
Safety cue: Keep front knee tracking over the second toe and chest lifted

Notification: Dumbbell Sumo Squat: Add resistance to inner-thigh focused squats—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Knee Pain / Knee Injury, Hip Pain (moderate–severe), Acute Low Back Pain
Health benefit: Strengthens inner thighs and glutes while improving lower-body stability.
Steps to perform:
1. Stand with feet wider than shoulder-width and hold a dumbbell vertically with both hands.
2. Lower into a squat by bending hips and knees while keeping chest upright.
3. Push through heels to return to standing position.
Safety cue: Keep chest tall and knees tracking over toes.

Notification: Goblet Squat: Improve squat depth with front-loaded support—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Knee Pain / Knee Injury, Acute Low Back Pain, Hip Pain (moderate–severe)
Health benefit: Builds full lower-body strength and core engagement.
Steps to perform:
1. Hold a dumbbell vertically at chest level with feet shoulder-width apart.
2. Lower into a squat by bending hips and knees while keeping chest upright.
3. Push through heels to return to standing.
Safety cue: Sit back into heels and keep spine neutral.

Notification: Dumbbell Rear Delt Fly Seated: Target rear delts for balanced shoulder strength—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Shoulder Impingement, Acute Rotator Cuff Tear, Acute Low Back Pain
Health benefit: Strengthens rear shoulders and improves posture.
Steps to perform:
1. Sit on a bench holding dumbbells and hinge forward at the hips with a neutral spine.
2. Raise arms out to the sides with a slight elbow bend, squeezing shoulder blades.
3. Lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting position.
Safety cue: Keep back straight and avoid swinging the weights.
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Shoulder Surgery, Severe Shoulder Arthritis (flare-up)

Notification: Standing Knee Drive: Improve hip power and coordination—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Knee Pain / Knee Injury, Hip Pain (moderate–severe), Ankle Instability (acute)
Health benefit: Enhances hip flexor strength and balance.
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and core engaged.
2. Drive one knee toward chest while balancing on the opposite leg.
3. Lower with control and alternate sides rhythmically.
Safety cue: Engage core and avoid leaning backward.
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Severe Balance Disorders, Uncontrolled Cardiac Conditions

Notification: Inner Range Quad: Strengthen quadriceps in terminal knee range—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Knee Pain (severe), Acute Patellar Tendon Injury
Health benefit: Activates and strengthens inner quadriceps for knee stability.
Steps to perform:
1. Lie on your back with a rolled towel under one knee.
2. Straighten the knee by lifting the foot until the leg is fully extended.
3. Lower slowly back to the starting position.
Safety cue: Keep knee aligned and move in a controlled range.
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Knee Surgery (early phase), Acute Ligament Tear

Notification: Elbow Circles: Improve elbow joint mobility and control—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Elbow Pain, Acute Shoulder Impingement
Health benefit: Improves shoulder mobility and joint lubrication.
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall with hands placed lightly on your shoulders.
2. Move elbows in slow circular motions forward.
3. Reverse direction and repeat with control.
Safety cue: Keep movements slow and pain-free.
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Elbow Surgery, Severe Shoulder Arthritis (flare-up)

Notification: Tibialis Raises Wall Lean: Strengthen shin muscles and ankle control—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Ankle Pain, Severe Shin Splints
Health benefit: Strengthens shin muscles and supports ankle stability.
Steps to perform:
1. Stand with your back against a wall and feet slightly forward.
2. Lift your toes toward your shins while keeping heels on the ground.
3. Lower the toes slowly back to the starting position.
Safety cue: Keep heels grounded and lift toes with control.
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Ankle Surgery, Acute Stress Fracture in the Lower Leg

Notification: Wall Push-Ups: Build chest and arm strength with wall support—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Shoulder Pain, Acute Wrist Pain
Health benefit: Builds upper-body strength with low joint stress.
Steps to perform:
1. Stand facing a wall with hands placed on the wall at shoulder height and shoulder-width apart.
2. Bend your elbows and lean your chest toward the wall while keeping your body in a straight line.
3. Push through your hands to straighten the arms and return to the starting position.
Safety cue: Keep body straight and avoid sagging hips.
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Shoulder Surgery, Severe Shoulder Arthritis (flare-up)

Notification: Table Slide Flexion: Improve shoulder flexion mobility safely—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Shoulder Pain, Acute Shoulder Impingement
Health benefit: Improves shoulder flexion range of motion and joint mobility
Steps to perform:
1. Sit facing a table with your hands resting on the surface and elbows slightly bent.
2. Slide your hands forward along the table while lowering your chest toward the floor.
3. Return slowly to the starting position with control.
Safety cue: Slide gently without forcing into pain.
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Shoulder Surgery, Acute Rotator Cuff Tear

Notification: World's Greatest Stretch: Mobilize hips, hamstrings, and thoracic spine—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Knee Pain, Acute Hip Pain
Health benefit: Enhances full-body mobility and hip flexibility.
Steps to perform:
1. Step one foot forward into a deep lunge with both hands on the floor inside the front foot.
2. Rotate your torso and reach one arm upward toward the ceiling.
3. Return the hand to the floor, step back, and repeat on the other side.
Safety cue: Move slowly and keep front knee aligned.
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Hip Surgery, Severe Balance Disorders

Notification: Lateral Toe Taps: Improve lateral footwork coordination—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Boosts coordination and lateral hip activation.
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and hands on hips or by your sides.
2. Tap one foot out to the side while keeping most of your weight on the standing leg.
3. Bring the foot back to center and alternate sides in a steady rhythm
Safety cue: Stay light on feet and keep core engaged.
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Severe Balance Disorders, Recent Ankle Surgery

Notification: Heel Digs: Activate hamstrings with rhythmic stepping—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Hamstring Strain, Acute Knee Pain
Health benefit: Activates hamstrings and improves lower-body coordination.
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and arms relaxed by your sides.
2. Extend one heel forward and lightly tap it on the floor while keeping the toe lifted.
3. Return to the starting position and alternate sides rhythmically.
Safety cue: Keep chest upright and land softly on heel.
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Severe Balance Disorders, Recent Hamstring Surgery

Notification: Plank Saw: Increase core tension with forward-back plank shifts—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Shoulder Pain, Acute Low Back Pain
Health benefit: Strengthens core stability and shoulder endurance.
Steps to perform:
1. Start in a forearm plank position with body in a straight line.
2. Slowly shift your body forward and backward while maintaining a strong plank.
3. Continue the controlled rocking motion without letting the hips drop.
Safety cue: Maintain neutral spine and avoid hip sag.
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Abdominal Surgery, Severe Shoulder Arthritis

Notification: Dumbbell Front Raise: Strengthen anterior delts with controlled lift—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Strengthens front shoulders and improves arm control.
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand with arms resting in front of your thighs.
2. Raise the dumbbells forward to shoulder height with a slight bend in the elbows.
3. Lower the weights slowly back to the starting position with control.
Safety cue: Lift to shoulder height without arching back.
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Shoulder Surgery, Severe Shoulder Arthritis (flare-up)

Notification: Lying Toe Taps: Strengthen lower abdominals with controlled leg taps—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Low Back Pain, Hip Flexor Strain
Health benefit: Builds lower abdominal strength and pelvic control.
Steps to perform:
1. Lie on your back with hips and knees bent at 90 degrees and arms by your sides.
2. Lower one foot to lightly tap the floor while keeping the core engaged.
3. Return to the starting position and alternate legs with control.
Safety cue: Keep lower back pressed into the floor.
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Abdominal Surgery, Lumbar Disc Herniation (acute phase)

Notification: Quadruped Rocking: Improve hip mobility in a supported position—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Wrist Pain, Acute Knee Pain
Health benefit: Improves hip mobility and core coordination.
Steps to perform:
1. Start on all fours with hands under shoulders and knees under hips.
2. Slowly rock your hips backward toward your heels while keeping arms extended.
3. Return to the starting position with control and repeat.
Safety cue: Keep spine neutral and move within comfort.
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Knee Surgery, Acute Lumbar Disc Herniation

Notification: Standing Punch Across Boxing Stance: Train rotational power and core control—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Shoulder Pain, Acute Elbow Pain
Health benefit: Enhances core rotation and upper-body endurance.
Steps to perform:
1. Stand in a staggered boxing stance with knees slightly bent and hands up near your chin.
2. Rotate your torso and extend one arm across your body in a controlled punching motion.
3. Retract the arm and alternate punches while maintaining your stance.
Safety cue: Rotate through torso, not just the arms.
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Shoulder Surgery, Severe Balance Disorders

Notification: Scaption Thumbs Up: Strengthen shoulders in a safe lifting plane—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Shoulder Pain, Acute Shoulder Impingement
Health benefit: Strengthens shoulder stabilizers and supports joint health.
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall with arms at your sides and thumbs pointing upward.
2. Raise your arms diagonally forward at about a 30–45° angle from your body.
3. Lower the arms slowly back to the starting position with control.
Safety cue: Raise arms at 45° with thumbs pointing up.
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Shoulder Surgery, Acute Rotator Cuff Tear

Notification: Standing Torso Rotation: Improve controlled trunk rotation—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Low Back Pain, Acute Hip Pain
Health benefit: Improves spinal mobility and core activation.
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and arms extended in front of you.
2. Rotate your torso gently to one side while keeping hips facing forward.
3. Return to center and rotate to the opposite side with control.
Safety cue: Rotate gently without twisting knees.
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Spinal Surgery, Acute Lumbar Disc Herniation

Notification: Wall Sit: Build quadriceps endurance with a static hold—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Knee Pain, Acute Hip Pain
Health benefit: Builds isometric strength in quads and glutes.
Steps to perform:
1. Stand with your back against a wall and feet about shoulder-width apart.
2. Slide down the wall until your knees are bent roughly 90 degrees.
3. Hold the position while keeping your back flat against the wall.
Safety cue: Keep knees at 90° and back flat against wall.
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Knee Surgery, Severe Knee Osteoarthritis flare-up

Notification: Reverse Plank Tabletop: Strengthen posterior chain and shoulder stability—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Wrist Pain, Acute Shoulder Pain
Health benefit: Strengthens posterior chain and opens chest.
Steps to perform:
1. Sit with knees bent, feet flat, and hands placed behind you on the floor.
2. Press through your hands and feet to lift your hips until the torso forms a tabletop position.
3. Hold briefly while keeping shoulders open and hips lifted.
Safety cue: Keep hips lifted and shoulders away from ears.
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Shoulder Surgery, Severe Wrist Arthritis

Notification: Seated Hamstring Stretch: Improve back-of-thigh flexibility—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Hamstring Strain, Acute Low Back Pain
Health benefit: Improves hamstring flexibility and reduces stiffness.
Steps to perform:
1. Sit on the floor with one leg extended and the other foot placed near the inner thigh.
2. Lean forward from the hips toward the extended leg while keeping the back straight.
3. Hold the stretch gently and breathe steadily before switching sides.
Safety cue: Hinge at hips and avoid rounding back.
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Hamstring Surgery, Acute Lumbar Disc Herniation

Notification: Dumbbell Bent Over Row: Build mid-back pulling strength—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Low Back Pain, Acute Shoulder Pain
Health benefit: Strengthens back muscles and improves posture.
Steps to perform:
1. Hinge forward at the hips with a neutral spine while holding dumbbells in both hands.
2. Pull the weights toward your torso by driving elbows back.
3. Lower the dumbbells slowly to the starting position with control.
Safety cue: Keep spine neutral and pull elbows toward hips.
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Spinal Surgery, Severe Shoulder Injury

Notification: Reverse Fly Bodyweight: Strengthen rear shoulders without equipment—tap to begin.
Physical limitation: Acute Shoulder Impingement, Acute Upper Back Pain
Health benefit: Activates rear shoulders and upper back without load.
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall and hinge slightly forward at the hips with arms extended in front.
2. Open your arms out to the sides by squeezing your shoulder blades together.
3. Return arms slowly to the starting position with control.
Safety cue: Squeeze shoulder blades without shrugging.
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Shoulder Surgery, Severe Shoulder Arthritis (flare-up)

Notification: Squat with Calf Raise: Combine lower-body strength with ankle power—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Builds lower-body strength and ankle power.
Steps to perform:
1. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and perform a squat by bending hips and knees.
2. Rise back to standing and lift your heels to perform a calf raise.
3. Lower heels and repeat the movement with control.
Safety cue: Keep balance centered and rise onto toes slowly.
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Recent Knee or Ankle Surgery, Severe Balance Disorders

Notification: Step Touch with Reach: Improve coordination with upper-body reach—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Improves coordination and full-body mobility.
Steps to perform:
1. Stand tall and step one foot to the side while reaching both arms overhead.
2. Bring the other foot to meet it while lowering the arms.
3. Repeat side-to-side in a rhythmic motion.
Safety cue: Move rhythmically and avoid locking knees.
is_not_suitable_for (Medical conditions): Severe Balance Disorders, Recent Shoulder Surgery

Notification: Step-Up: Build single-leg strength and improve knee stability—tap to begin.
Health benefit: Strengthens glutes and quads while improving balance and functional lower-body strength.
Safety cue: Place full foot on the step and drive through the heel without pushing off the back leg.
